name: Black Bean Patties with Air-Fried Portobello Mushrooms
description: Smoky, spiced black bean patties air-fried until golden, served in buns with air-fried Portobello mushrooms.

ingredients.name: breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: add an extra tbsp if mixture is too wet

ingredients.name: chipotle paste
ingredients.notes: or a pinch of chilli flakes

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: for brushing patties before air frying

steps.title: Soften the veg
steps.content: Heat {0014} in a frying pan over medium heat. Sauté {0002} and {0004} for 5–6 minutes until softened. Add {0003} and cook for another minute. Set aside to cool slightly.

steps.title: Mash the beans
steps.content: Tip {0001} into a large bowl. Mash roughly two-thirds with a fork or potato masher, leaving the rest whole for texture.

steps.title: Mix the patty mixture
steps.content: Add the softened veg to the beans along with {0005}, {0006}, {0007}, {0008}, {0009}, {0010}, {0011}, {0012}, and {0013}. Mix well until combined. The mixture should hold its shape when pressed — if too wet, add another tablespoon of breadcrumbs.

steps.title: Shape and chill
steps.content: Divide the mixture into 4–5 patties, pressing firmly to compact them. Place on a plate, cover, and refrigerate for at least 20–30 minutes to help them hold together during cooking.

steps.title: Prep the mushrooms
steps.content: Mix {0017}, {0018}, and {0019} together. Brush over both sides of {0016}. Set aside until ready to cook.

steps.title: Air fry the patties
steps.content: Preheat your air fryer to 190°C. Brush both sides of the patties lightly with {0015}. Cook for 12–15 minutes, flipping carefully halfway through, until golden and firm on the outside.

steps.title: Air fry the mushrooms
steps.content: While the patties rest, cook {0016} at 190°C for 8–10 minutes, gill-side up for the first half, until tender and slightly caramelised.

steps.title: Assemble and serve
steps.content: Toast {0020} lightly. Layer with a patty, a Portobello mushroom, and any toppings you like — smashed avocado, sliced tomato, or shredded lettuce all work well.

notes:
Makes 4–5 patties; base_servings set to 2 (the stated yield), with patty count range noted here.

Leftover patties keep in the fridge for 3 days; reheat in the air fryer at 180°C for 5–6 minutes. To freeze: shape before cooking, freeze on a tray until solid, then bag — keeps for up to 3 months.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'black-bean-patties-portobello-mushrooms' used verbatim; not re-derived.
_fixture_notes.dietary_category: Source labels the dish 'Vegetarian · Dairy-free · Egg-free'. All ingredients are plant-based; classified 'vegan' per the least-restrictive-eater rule. The source's own labelling omits 'vegan' but no animal-derived ingredients are present.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Gluten: breadcrumbs and burger buns. Soy: soy sauce (both groups) and white miso paste. No dairy, eggs, nuts, peanuts, shellfish, or sesame detected.
_fixture_notes.cuisine: No strong single-cuisine identity — spicing draws on Mexican and broader fusion influences. Left null.
_fixture_notes.meal_types: No explicit meal-type framing in source beyond a burger/patty format. Assigned dinner and lunch as the most natural fits. No breakfast framing.
_fixture_notes.base_servings: Source states 'SERVES 2 (makes 4–5 patties)'. Serves 2 is the canonical yield. Patty count is a range — lower bound 4 noted; range recorded in notes per range-quantity convention.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: Stated step durations: soften veg 5–6 min (lower bound 5), garlic 1 min, chill 20–30 min (in-session passive, lower bound 20), air fry patties 12–15 min (lower bound 12), air fry mushrooms 8–10 min (lower bound 8). Steps 6 and 7 run sequentially — mushrooms cook while patties rest, not simultaneously with active cooking. However, the 20–30 min chill (step 4) is an in-session passive wait under 2 hours, so it counts toward cook time per the spec. Total if sequential: 5+1+20+12+8 = 46 min. But step 5 (prep mushrooms) runs during or after the chill with no stated duration overlap, and the transition between chill and frying is ambiguous. The spec requires critical-path confidence; given the sequential structure is reasonably clear here, a sum of stated durations is defensible — but prep_time_minutes has no labelled field either. Both fields left null to stay conservative; individual durations are captured in timer_seconds.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: No labelled prep field. Left null per stated-times-only convention.
_fixture_notes.olive_oil_duplicates: Olive oil appears in three distinct uses: for softening veg (0014, 1 tbsp, patties group), for brushing patties (0015, functional amount, patties group), and for the mushroom marinade (0019, 1 tbsp, mushrooms group). Three separate rows per the duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention.
_fixture_notes.soy_sauce_duplicate: Soy sauce appears in both the patty mixture (0011) and mushroom marinade (0018). Separate rows per duplicate-ingredient-across-groups convention.
_fixture_notes.garlic_duplicate: Garlic appears in both the patty soften step (0003, 3 cloves) and mushroom marinade (0017, 2 cloves). Separate rows.
_fixture_notes.chipotle_paste_alternative: Source lists 'chipotle paste (or pinch of chilli flakes)'. Inline-alternatives convention applied: chipotle paste taken as canonical (0009), alternative noted in ingredient notes.
_fixture_notes.fresh_coriander: Source lists '3 tbsp fresh coriander, roughly chopped'. Leading-prep-modifier convention: 'roughly chopped' is a prep verb, so converted to trailing form: 'fresh coriander, roughly chopped'. 'Fresh' is a canonical-identity modifier and stays.
_fixture_notes.tin_weight: Source specifies '1 tin black beans, drained and rinsed'. Tin-weight convention applied: 400g used as amount. Tin format noted in ingredient notes.
_fixture_notes.burger_buns: Buns contain gluten; already captured in dietary_restrictions. No further action needed.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. The mushroom marinade/cook is a potential bento or component_dinner veg candidate; flagged for operator review.
